title: Postman tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Postman API key detected - CRITICAL. Postman API keys (PMAK-*) provide programmatic access to Postman's API platform, granting full control over: workspaces, collections, environments, monitors, mock servers, and API documentation. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized users to read/modify/delete your API collections, access sensitive environment variables, modify mock servers, access API usage data, and potentially leak API request/response data stored in Postman. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Revoke this API key immediately from Postman Settings > API Keys, 2) Remove the key from source code, 3) Generate a new API key with minimal required scopes, 4) Store securely in environment variables (POSTMAN_API_KEY) or secrets managers (AWS/Vault/Azure), 5) Review Postman workspace access logs for unauthorized activity, 6) Audit all collections and environments for sensitive data exposure, 7) Implement API key rotation policy.

Postman API key detected in variable assignment - CRITICAL. Hardcoded Postman API key found in variable assignment. These keys provide full access to Postman workspaces, collections, environments, and monitors. Exposing API keys allows unauthorized access to: view/modify/delete API collections, read environment variables (which may contain other secrets), manipulate mock servers, access API testing history, and compromise your entire API development workflow. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Remove hardcoded key from source code, 2) Revoke compromised key via Postman Account Settings > API Keys, 3) Generate new API key with restricted scopes, 4) Store in environment variables (POSTMAN_API_KEY), 5) Use secrets managers for production deployments, 6) Review workspace activity logs, 7) Audit all collections for sensitive data.
